title: A simple IELTS Writing Task 2 structure
excerpt: Use this four-paragraph structure when you need a clear, fast plan for Task 2.
body:
## The basic shape

Most IELTS Task 2 essays can be handled with four clear paragraphs:

- Introduction with your position
- Body paragraph 1 with your first main idea
- Body paragraph 2 with your second main idea
- Conclusion that answers the question directly

## Why it works

This structure keeps your argument easy to follow. It also helps you avoid spending too much time on planning when you need to write under exam conditions.

## Quick practice

Take one question and write only the topic sentence for each body paragraph. If the topic sentences clearly answer the prompt, the full essay will usually be easier to finish.

title: How to practise IELTS Speaking Part 2
excerpt: A simple routine for building longer, more natural cue card answers.
body:
## Use a repeatable plan

For most cue cards, prepare your answer in four moves:

- Say what the topic is
- Give one or two details
- Tell a short story or example
- Explain why it matters to you

## Time yourself

Practise speaking for one minute first. Then repeat the same card and stretch the answer to two minutes by adding examples, feelings, and comparisons.

## Review one thing at a time

Do not try to fix every mistake in one recording. Listen again and choose one focus: fluency, grammar, pronunciation, or vocabulary.
